description: Loading with blind append/insert so a retried or re-run task double-inserts rows and corrupts aggregates.
body: Failure mode: a load task appends rows without partition scoping or upsert, so when the orchestrator retries after a partial failure the same rows are written twice and every downstream sum/count is inflated. Detection signals: distinct-key count is less than total count; aggregates jump after a retry; the load uses INSERT with no replace/merge. Do not append into a target that can be retried. Safer fallback: scope the write to a partition and replace-by-partition or upsert on a key, then verify a double-run yields identical counts.
